spec_match.rationale: The live preview and generated CSS now use the CSS Color 5 alpha(from color / percentage) form when supported, with rgba() fallback values otherwise.

interactive.rationale: Interactive color inputs update the generated tokens, support status, CSS block, and preview components in real time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page explains and demonstrates a design-token builder that derives opacity tiers from single base colors.

edge_cases.rationale: The page detects native alpha() support, names the active rendering mode, and falls back to equivalent rgba() previews and copyable fallback tokens when unsupported.

references.rationale: The concept page cites both the ChromeStatus entry and the CSS Color 5 alpha() section.

design_system.rationale: New styling uses shared CSS variables, preview button foregrounds are set with their background overrides, and generated ink tokens preserve contrast for chosen base colors.

summary: The previously reported opacity math and unused alphaColor helper issues are resolved; the concept now has native alpha() preview paths, an explicit unsupported-browser fallback, and no remaining open critique questions.
